concepts.title: bond
concepts.definition: tradable debt instrument representing a loan in which the issuer owes the holder(s) a debt

concepts.title: bond coupon
concepts.definition: interest rate on a debt security that the issuer promises to pay to the holder until maturity, expressed as an annual percentage of the face value

concepts.title: callable bond
concepts.definition: bond that includes a stipulation allowing the issuer the right to repurchase and retire the bond at the call price after the call protection period

concepts.title: commercial paper
concepts.definition: an unsecured short-term debt instrument typically issued by a bank, corporation, or foreign government to obtain funds to meet short-term debt obligations, such as accounts receivable, inventories, or payroll, backed only by an issuing bank or company promise to pay the face amount on the maturity date specified on the note

concepts.title: convertible bond
concepts.definition: bond that gives the holder the right to convert the bond into a fixed number of shares (conversion ratio) if the equity price rises above a specified level (strike price)

concepts.title: corporate bond
concepts.definition: bond issued by a company in order to raise financing

concepts.title: fixed coupon bond
concepts.definition: bond whose coupon rate and principal amount are fixed at the time of origination or sale and remain constant while the security is outstanding

concepts.title: floating rate note
concepts.definition: bond with a variable interest rate based on a published reference interest rate

concepts.title: government bond
concepts.definition: debt security issued by a government to fund government spending

concepts.title: municipal bond
concepts.definition: government bond that may be issued by a regional, rather than national, authority

concepts.title: sovereign debt instrument
concepts.definition: debt security issued by the government of a country

concepts.title: treasury bill
concepts.definition: short-term zero coupon treasury obligation with a maturity ranging from one to twelve months

concepts.title: zero coupon bond
concepts.definition: bond issued with a coupon rate of zero and that trades at a deep discount to face value

concepts.title: American depositary receipt
concepts.definition: depositary receipt that represents securities of a foreign company and allows that company's securities to trade in the U.S. financial markets

concepts.title: common share
concepts.definition: share that signifies a unit of ownership in a corporation and represents a claim on part of the corporation's assets and earnings

concepts.title: depositary receipt
concepts.definition: negotiable financial instrument issued by a trust company, security depositary, or bank that is evidence of the deposit of publicly traded securities and that facilitates the ownership of securities traded in other jurisdictions

concepts.title: dividend
concepts.definition: announced commitment to make a specific distribution of a portion of earnings to shareholders, prorated by class of security

concepts.title: fixed rate dividend
concepts.definition: dividend that provides a specified annual return on the nominal value (and any premium) paid on shares

concepts.title: global depositary receipt
concepts.definition: depositary receipt where a certificate issued by a depositary bank, which purchases securities of foreign companies, creates a security on a local exchange backed by those securities

concepts.title: non-voting share
concepts.definition: share that has no voting right

concepts.title: partially paid share
concepts.definition: share whose payment status indicates that only a portion of the market value has been received by the company for the shares

concepts.title: preferred share
concepts.definition: share that pays dividends at a specified rate and has preference over common shares in the payment of dividends and liquidation of corporate assets

concepts.title: share yield
concepts.definition: ratio of the annualized dividend per share divided by the (current) price per share

concepts.title: collective investment vehicle
concepts.definition: assets pooled by investors whose share capital remains separate from the assets of the vehicle

concepts.title: equity fund
concepts.definition: A fund which invests in at least 85% shares.

concepts.title: exchange-traded fund
concepts.definition: investment fund whose fund units are traded on an exchange, much like stocks

concepts.title: fund unit
concepts.definition: quantified share of beneficial interest in a pooled fund, representing a proportional claim on the fund's assets, income, or entitlements

concepts.title: hedge fund
concepts.definition: investment fund that pursues a total return and is usually open to qualified investors only

concepts.title: mutual fund
concepts.definition: open-end professionally managed investment fund established for the purpose of investing in securities such as stocks, bonds, money market instruments and similar assets

concepts.title: basket of securities
concepts.definition: basket composed of securities, typically of a particular asset class such as equities or bonds

concepts.title: international securities identification number
concepts.definition: security identifier that is defined as specified in ISO 6166, Securities and related financial instruments -- International securities identification numbering system (ISIN)

concepts.title: listing
concepts.definition: catalog entry for a securities offering managed by an exchange that provides the terms under which that security is made available on that exchange

concepts.title: portfolio
concepts.definition: collection of investments (financial assets) such as stocks, bonds and cash equivalents, as well as mutual funds, real estate, and so forth

concepts.title: redemption event
concepts.definition: a prescriptive event involving the return of an investor's principal in a fixed-income security, such as a preferred stock or bond, or the sale of units in a mutual fund

concepts.title: redemption schedule
concepts.definition: repayment schedule whereby a given percentage of a bond issue is redeemed on predefined dates

concepts.title: security registry
concepts.definition: registry used to manage security identifiers and related information

concepts.title: weighted average maturity
concepts.definition: weighted average amount of time until the maturities on mortgages in a mortgage-backed security (MBS)

concepts.title: yield
concepts.definition: return on the investor's capital investment

concepts.title: issuer
concepts.definition: role of a party that issues (or proposes to issue in a formal filing) one or more financial instruments

concepts.title: m b s issuer
concepts.definition: The issuer of a Mortgage Backed Security.

concepts.title: prospectus
concepts.definition: formal, written offering document to sell securities that provides the facts an investor needs to make an informed investment decision

concepts.title: security underwriter
concepts.definition: party that has purchased from an issuer with a view to, or sells for an issuer in connection with, the distribution of any security, or participates or has a direct or indirect participation in any such undertaking, or participates or has a participation in the direct or indirect underwriting of any such undertaking

concepts.title: security underwriting arrangement
concepts.definition: underwriting agreement between an organization (typically an investment bank) and a securities issuer that commits the underwriter to assuming risk involved in buying a new issue of securities and reselling it to the public

concepts.title: share issuer
concepts.definition: issuer of securities that represent an ownership interest in something

concepts.title: underwriter
concepts.definition: financial service provider that evaluates and assumes another party's risk for a fee, such as a commission, premium, spread or interest

concepts.title: debt instrument
concepts.definition: financial instrument and credit agreement evidencing monies owed by the issuer to the holder on terms as specified

concepts.title: equity instrument
concepts.definition: financial instrument representing an ownership interest in an entity or pool of assets

concepts.title: listed security
concepts.definition: registered security listed on at least one exchange

concepts.title: security
concepts.definition: financial instrument that can be bought or sold

concepts.title: security form
concepts.definition: nature of the proof of ownership of a security

concepts.title: share
concepts.definition: financial instrument that signifies a unit of equity ownership in a corporation, or a unit of ownership in a mutual fund, or interest in a general or limited partnership, or a unit of ownership in a structured product, such as a real estate investment trust

concepts.title: tradable debt instrument
concepts.definition: a debt instrument that is also a security, i.e., that can be bought and sold by the holder

concepts.title: asset-backed security
concepts.definition: debt instrument backed by receivables other than those arising out of real estate loans or mortgages

concepts.title: c d o pool
concepts.definition: A pool of CDO securities. The underlying of the CDO Squared is a pool of CDO instruments.

concepts.title: collateralized debt obligation
concepts.definition: structured finance constructed from a portfolio of fixed income assets including corporate loans and mortgage backed securities. A special purpose vehicle (SPV) issues notes to investors in order to raise funds that are invested in a portfolio of those fixed income assets, held by the SPV as collateral for the notes. Further notes: Collateralized Debt Obligation, for example, ABS CDO which consists of a portfolio of different ABS bonds, and the payments to the holders of these trust certificates are derived from the cash flows of the ABS bonds. This CDO instrument is part of a CDO issue, consisting of individual CDO instruments of a given seniority. Often referred to as tranches and slices (Investopedia). Investopedia: Similar in structure to a collateralized mortgage obligation (CMO) or collateralized bond obligation (CBO), CDOs are unique in that they represent different types of debt and credit risk. In the case of CDOs, these different types of debt are often referred to as 'tranches' or 'slices'. Each slice has a different maturity and risk associated with it. The higher the risk, the more the CDO pays. Further details: Collateralized Debt obligations are securitized interests in pools of - generally non-mortgage - assets. Assets - called collateral - usually comprise loans or debt instruments. A CDO may be called a collateralized loan obligation (CLO) or collateralized bond obligation (CBO) if it holds only loans or bonds, respectively. Investors bear the credit risk of the collateral. Multiple tranches of securities are issued by the CDO, offering investors various maturity and credit risk characteristics.

concepts.title: m b s pool
concepts.definition: A pool investment consisting of a collection of MBS instruments.

concepts.title: mortgage-backed security
concepts.definition: debt obligations that represent claims to the cash flows from pools of mortgage loans, most commonly on residential property

concepts.title: pool
concepts.definition: combination of resources for a common purpose or benefit

concepts.title: tranche
concepts.definition: segment of a pool of securities, typically debt instruments

bridges.rationale: A mortgage-backed security represents claims on the cash flows from an underlying pool of mortgage loans. FIBO models the security (SEC) and the mortgage instrument (LOAN) in separate domains but does not draw the edge from the MBS to the mortgage loans that back it, which securitization and loan-level disclosure analysis need.
bridges.citation: SEC Regulation AB, 17 CFR 229.1100 (asset-backed securities, loan-level data)

bridges.rationale: An asset-backed security is collateralized by a pool of receivables such as auto or consumer loans. FIBO defines the ABS in SEC and the loan in LOAN but leaves the backing relationship between the security and its underlying loans implicit.
bridges.citation: SEC Regulation AB, 17 CFR 229.1100; securitization structuring

bridges.rationale: For securities issuance and regulatory reporting a securities issuer is identified by its Legal Entity Identifier. FIBO defines the Issuer role (FBC) and the LegalEntityIdentifier (BE) but does not connect an issuer to its LEI.
bridges.citation: ISO 17442 (LEI); MiFID II / ESMA and SEC issuer identification
